title: The engine of Germany's wealth is blocking its future
full_text: It's led to less innovation, losses in competitiveness, more emissions, and job cuts. Besides jobs, research and administration are also increasingly relocated to other countries. “What is lost does not come back quickly. Destroying viable industrial structures unnecessarily is irresponsible,” IG Metall, Germany's largest worker's union, told TEC. “This is a creeping catastrophe for the country in terms of prosperity and resilience.”
